chunks.title: Hamburg data availability
chunks.content:
# Hamburg Data Availability

City name: Hamburg.

Available variables: noise, light, tree, trafficLight, tactile_pavement, temperatureSummer, temperatureWinter, greeninf, blueinf, station, wcDisabled, narrowRoads, stair, obstacle, slope, unevenSurface, poorPavement, kerbsHigh, facility, pedestrianFlow.

Unavailable or limited variables: ramp is listed in city configuration but is not currently rendered as a selectable comfort variable in the sidebar. Treat ramp as limited unless a future UI and data layer explicitly support it.

Available map layers: noise_wms, temp_summer, temp_winter, streetlight, trafic_light_wms, tactile_guidance, tree_wms, green_infrastructure_wms, blue_infrastructure_wms, transport_station_wms, wc_disabled, sidewalk_narrow, stair, obstacle, slope, uneven_surfaces, poor_pavement, kerbs_high, facility_wms, pedestrian_flow_wms.

Warnings: Some layers are WMS visualization layers. The agent should not claim precise feature counts or local spatial conditions from RAG. Local spatial facts must come from CAT map state, `/api/accessibility`, or spatial summary tools.

chunks.title: Penteli data availability
chunks.content:
# Penteli Data Availability

City name: Penteli.

Available variables: trafficLight, greeninf, temperatureSummer, temperatureWinter, station, stair, slope, facility, pedestrianFlow.

Unavailable or limited variables: noise, light, tree, tactile_pavement, blueinf, wcDisabled, narrowRoads, obstacle, unevenSurface, poorPavement, kerbsHigh.

Available map layers: trafic_light, green_infrastructure, transport_station, stair, slope_penteli, facilities, pedestrian_flow, temp_summer, temp_winter.

Warnings: If a profile or user request includes an unavailable variable, the agent must remove it before proposing an action and include a missing data warning.

chunks.title: How to use CAT
chunks.content:
# How To Use CAT

Select a city, set walking time and walking speed, choose a start point, enable comfort variables, adjust their sensitivity, and run the catchment area analysis.

The agent can help by detecting a user profile from natural language and proposing CAT settings. The user should be able to review the profile, walking speed, walking time, enabled variables, unavailable variables, and action before analysis runs.

chunks.title: CAT troubleshooting
chunks.content:
# CAT Troubleshooting

If no reachable area appears, check that a start point is selected, walking time and walking speed are reasonable, and at least one supported city dataset is available.

If comfort-adjusted area is empty, reduce the sensitivity of selected discomfort variables or try fewer variables.

If a requested variable is not applied, it may not be available for the selected city.

chunks.title: CAT workflow
chunks.content:
# CAT Workflow

CAT lets users select a city, choose walking time and walking speed, place a start point, enable comfort variables, set variable sensitivity values, and run the existing accessibility analysis.

The accessibility agent should translate natural language into a structured action that updates existing CAT state. It should not create a separate map calculation flow.

Preferred flow: user message, intent detection, RAG retrieval, structured action generation, frontend state update, existing MapComponent analysis flow, then explanation based on actual result metadata.

chunks.title: Comfort weight logic
chunks.content:
# Comfort Weight Logic

CAT uses variable sensitivity values to adjust comfort-based walking accessibility. Lower values represent stronger discomfort or lower tolerance for that factor. Higher values represent weaker discomfort or higher tolerance.

The agent should use deterministic profile presets for final values. The LLM may detect a profile, but it should not freely invent walking speed or layer values.

Every suggested variable must be filtered against city availability before being applied.

chunks.title: Data limitations
chunks.content:
# Data Limitations

Data availability differs by city. A variable available in Hamburg may not be available in Penteli.

RAG knowledge can explain available datasets and limitations, but it cannot provide local spatial truth for a selected point or catchment area.

The frontend and APIs must show missing data warnings when requested or preset variables are filtered out.

chunks.title: Result interpretation
chunks.content:
# Result Interpretation

Result explanations must use actual result metadata, such as default reachable area, comfort-adjusted reachable area, comfort ratio, enabled variables, POI count, and missing data warnings.

The agent may explain that a smaller comfort-adjusted area suggests that selected comfort factors reduce comfortable walking accessibility.

The agent must not claim local physical causes, such as many stairs or poor pavement, unless that fact was computed or retrieved from an approved spatial tool.

chunks.title: Routing logic
chunks.content:
# Routing Logic

CAT accessibility analysis is based on the existing GIS and routing workflow. The agent must not invent routing results.

Road network geometry, reachable areas, catchment polygons, and route calculations must stay outside RAG. These spatial outputs should be produced by CAT APIs and GIS tools.

The agent may ask the frontend to run analysis after it proposes walking time, walking speed, enabled variables, layer values, city, and optionally coordinates.

chunks.title: Children and family profile
chunks.content:
# Children And Family Profile

Profile name: Children, families, and stroller users.

Typical walking speed: 3.5 km/h.

Relevant comfort variables: narrowRoads, kerbsHigh, stair, obstacle, poorPavement, unevenSurface, trafficLight, facility, greeninf, light.

Recommended variable values:
- narrowRoads: 0.4
- kerbsHigh: 0.4
- stair: 0.4
- obstacle: 0.5
- poorPavement: 0.5
- unevenSurface: 0.5
- trafficLight: 0.8
- facility: 0.8
- greeninf: 0.8
- light: 0.7

Explanation: Families and stroller users benefit from smooth surfaces, generous sidewalks, safe crossings, nearby facilities, green areas, and good lighting.

Warnings or limitations: The profile should be city-filtered before use because not every CAT city has all comfort variables.

chunks.title: Default adult profile
chunks.content:
# Default Adult Profile

Profile name: Default adult pedestrian.

Typical walking speed: 5.0 km/h.

Relevant comfort variables: light, trafficLight, station, facility, greeninf, noise.

Recommended variable values:
- light: 0.7
- trafficLight: 0.7
- station: 0.8
- facility: 0.7
- greeninf: 0.7
- noise: 0.5

Explanation: This is a neutral starting point for users who do not describe a specific mobility profile.

Warnings or limitations: The profile should stay conservative and should not imply a medical or disability-specific need.

chunks.title: Elderly profile
chunks.content:
# Elderly Profile

Profile name: Elderly person.

Typical walking speed: 3.0 km/h.

Relevant comfort variables: stair, slope, unevenSurface, poorPavement, kerbsHigh, obstacle, trafficLight, station, wcDisabled, light.

Recommended variable values:
- stair: 0.5
- slope: 0.5
- unevenSurface: 0.5
- poorPavement: 0.5
- kerbsHigh: 0.5
- obstacle: 0.5
- trafficLight: 0.7
- station: 0.9
- wcDisabled: 0.9
- light: 0.8

Explanation: Elderly pedestrians may walk more slowly and may be more sensitive to stairs, slopes, uneven surfaces, poor pavement, high kerbs, obstacles, poor lighting, and lack of nearby supportive infrastructure.

Warnings or limitations: These values are deterministic defaults for CAT. They should be filtered by city availability before being applied.

chunks.title: Visually impaired profile
chunks.content:
# Visually Impaired Profile

Profile name: Visually impaired pedestrian.

Typical walking speed: 4.0 km/h.

Relevant comfort variables: tactile_pavement, light, trafficLight, obstacle, pedestrianFlow.

Recommended variable values:
- tactile_pavement: 0.9
- light: 0.8
- trafficLight: 0.8
- obstacle: 0.4
- pedestrianFlow: 0.5

Explanation: Visually impaired pedestrians may rely on tactile paving, lighting, predictable crossings, and obstacle-free paths. Crowding may make navigation more stressful.

Warnings or limitations: The agent must not claim that tactile guidance exists unless the selected city supports the relevant layer or the result metadata includes it.

chunks.title: Wheelchair user profile
chunks.content:
# Wheelchair User Profile

Profile name: Wheelchair user.

Typical walking speed: 3.0 km/h.

Relevant comfort variables: stair, kerbsHigh, slope, narrowRoads, poorPavement, unevenSurface, obstacle, wcDisabled, trafficLight.

Recommended variable values:
- stair: 0.1
- kerbsHigh: 0.2
- slope: 0.3
- narrowRoads: 0.3
- poorPavement: 0.3
- unevenSurface: 0.3
- obstacle: 0.3
- wcDisabled: 0.9
- trafficLight: 0.7

Explanation: Wheelchair users are strongly affected by stairs, high kerbs, steep slopes, narrow sidewalks, damaged pavement, uneven surfaces, and obstacles. Accessible toilets and safe crossings can support independent movement.

Warnings or limitations: The profile should not assume ramps or barrier-free routes unless those facts are available from actual CAT data or result metadata.

chunks.title: RAG data boundary
chunks.content:
# RAG Data Boundary

The CAT accessibility agent uses RAG only for textual knowledge and metadata.

Allowed knowledge includes project methodology, user profile definitions, comfort variable explanations, city data availability, FAQ, UI guidance, and map layer metadata such as layer names or availability.

RAG must not contain raw geometry, road network geometries, POI point coordinates, city boundary coordinates, routing result GeoJSON, or catchment area polygons.

Spatial facts must come from the existing CAT Web GIS workflow, including frontend map state, `/api/accessibility`, Turf/PostGIS/pgRouting outputs, and actual result metadata.

The LLM should propose structured actions and explanations. It must not directly manipulate React state, invent map results, or generate raw GeoJSON.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: noise
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: noise
Meaning: Traffic or environmental noise that may make walking stressful.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: default_adult, elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: noise, loud streets, traffic noise, noisy area.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.5 to 0.7.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: light
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: light
Meaning: Missing or insufficient street lighting.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly, visually_impaired, children_family, default_adult.
Common user expressions: night, lighting, dark street, safe after dark.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.7 to 0.8.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: tree
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: tree
Meaning: Lack of tree shade.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly, children_family, default_adult.
Common user expressions: shade, trees, heat, sun exposure.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.6 to 0.8.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: trafficLight
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: trafficLight
Meaning: Missing signalized crossing support.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly, wheelchair_user, visually_impaired, children_family.
Common user expressions: crossing, traffic light, signal, safe crossing.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.7 to 0.8.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: tactile_pavement
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: tactile_pavement
Meaning: Missing tactile paving or tactile guidance.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: visually_impaired.
Common user expressions: tactile paving, blind guidance, tactile path.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.8 to 0.9.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: temperatureSummer
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: temperatureSummer
Meaning: Hot weather or heat exposure.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: hot weather, summer heat, heat stress.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.6 to 0.8.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: temperatureWinter
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: temperatureWinter
Meaning: Cold weather or winter discomfort.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly.
Common user expressions: winter, cold, icy conditions.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.5 to 0.7.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: greeninf
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: greeninf
Meaning: Lack of green infrastructure or green space.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: children_family, default_adult.
Common user expressions: green space, park, pleasant walking.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.7 to 0.8.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: blueinf
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: blueinf
Meaning: Lack of blue infrastructure or water features.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: default_adult, children_family.
Common user expressions: waterfront, water, blue space.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.6 to 0.7.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: station
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: station
Meaning: Poor access to public transport stations.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly, default_adult.
Common user expressions: bus stop, train station, public transport.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.8 to 0.9.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: wcDisabled
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: wcDisabled
Meaning: Missing accessible toilets.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly.
Common user expressions: accessible toilet, wheelchair toilet, restroom.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.8 to 0.9.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: narrowRoads
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: narrowRoads
Meaning: Narrow sidewalks or paths.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, children_family.
Common user expressions: narrow sidewalk, narrow path, stroller, wheelchair passage.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.3 to 0.5.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: stair
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: stair
Meaning: Stairs or step barriers.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: stairs, steps, no ramp.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.1 to 0.5.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: obstacle
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: obstacle
Meaning: Obstacles or blockages.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly, visually_impaired, children_family.
Common user expressions: obstacle, blockage, barrier.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.3 to 0.6.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: slope
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: slope
Meaning: Slopes or steep inclines.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: slope, hill, steep street.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.3 to 0.6.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: unevenSurface
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: unevenSurface
Meaning: Uneven or irregular walking surface.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: uneven surface, cobblestone, rough surface.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.3 to 0.6.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: poorPavement
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: poorPavement
Meaning: Damaged or poor pavement quality.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: poor pavement, damaged road, broken sidewalk.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.3 to 0.6.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: kerbsHigh
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: kerbsHigh
Meaning: High kerbs or abrupt curb transitions.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: wheelchair_user, elderly, children_family.
Common user expressions: high kerb, curb, raised kerb.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.2 to 0.5.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: facility
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: facility
Meaning: Lack of nearby facilities such as shops, benches, or services.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: elderly, children_family, default_adult.
Common user expressions: facilities, shops, benches, rest areas.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.7 to 0.8.

chunks.title: CAT comfort variables: pedestrianFlow
chunks.content:
# CAT Comfort Variables
Variable key: pedestrianFlow
Meaning: Crowded or high pedestrian flow areas.
Type: negative factor.
Relevant profiles: visually_impaired, elderly.
Common user expressions: crowded, busy, pedestrian flow.
Recommended sensitivity: 0.5 to 0.7.
